topics.topic_name: Can all changes always be reversed?

cards.question: Everything in the universe undergoes a change. Some changes occurring are natural. For example, seasons change, the formation of rocks, the formation of glaciers, and the flowering of plants. Some changes are man-made, for example, cooking of food, burning of oil, weaving of cloth, and producing food grains.
cards.text_html:
• Everything in the universe undergoes a change.
  • Some changes occurring are natural. For example, seasons change, the formation of rocks, the formation of glaciers, and the flowering of plants.
  • Some changes are man-made, for example, cooking of food, burning of oil, weaving of cloth, and producing food grains.

cards.question: Causes of Change Heating Applying force Mixing it with something else.
cards.text_html:
Causes of Change
  • Heating
  • Applying force
  • Mixing it with something else.

cards.question: Changes caused by heating Some objects get hot but do not change in any other way. Some objects get hot and also expand in size. Some objects get hot and begin to burn. Some objects get hot and change their state.
cards.text_html:
Changes caused by heating
  • Some objects get hot but do not change in any other way.
  • Some objects get hot and also expand in size.
  • Some objects get hot and begin to burn.
  • Some objects get hot and change their state.

cards.question: Changes by applying pressure We can change its shape and size. Air can be compressed. Metals can be hammered into thin sheets. Elastic can be stretched. Cotton can be spun into thin threads.
cards.text_html:
Changes by applying pressure
  • We can change its shape and size.
  • Air can be compressed.
  • Metals can be hammered into thin sheets.
  • Elastic can be stretched.
  • Cotton can be spun into thin threads.

cards.question: Physical Change When no new substance is formed after the change, it is called a physical change. Examples: Melting of ice, drying of wet clothes, tearing paper into pieces, chopping of wood, melting of wax, etc. Physical changes are usually reversible.
cards.text_html:
Physical Change 
  • When no new substance is formed after the change, it is called a physical change. Examples: Melting of ice, drying of wet clothes, tearing paper into pieces, chopping of wood, melting of wax, etc.
  • Physical changes are usually reversible.

cards.question: Chemical Change When a new substance is formed after the change, it is called a chemical change. Chemical changes cannot be reversed by simple physical means. Examples: Burning of candles, digesting of food, rusting of iron, respiration, cooking of food, formation of compost, etc.
cards.text_html:
Chemical Change
  • When a new substance is formed after the change, it is called a chemical change. Chemical changes cannot be reversed by simple physical means.
  • Examples: Burning of candles, digesting of food, rusting of iron, respiration, cooking of food, formation of compost, etc.

cards.question: Some changes ​​​​​​​
cards.text_html:
Some changes

​​​​​​​

cards.question: Characteristics of physical changes: No new substances are formed. Products are identical to the reactants. These changes are reversible.
cards.text_html:
Characteristics of physical changes:
  • No new substances are formed.
  • Products are identical to the reactants.
  • These changes are reversible.

cards.question: Characteristics of chemical changes: The properties of products are different from the properties of reactants. Most of the chemical changes are irreversible. These changes always result in energy changes.
cards.text_html:
Characteristics of chemical changes: 
  • The properties of products are different from the properties of reactants.
  • Most of the chemical changes are irreversible.
  • These changes always result in energy changes.

topics.topic_name: Could there be other ways to bring a change?

cards.question: Reversible changes : These are the changes that can be reversed. For example, stretching of rubber.
cards.text_html: Reversible changes: These are the changes that can be reversed. For example, stretching of rubber.

cards.question: ​​​​​​​ Irreversible changes: These are the changes that cannot be brought back to their original state. For example, the burning of paper.
cards.text_html: ​​​​​​​Irreversible changes: These are the changes that cannot be brought back to their original state. For example, the burning of paper.

cards.question: Melting point: A constant temperature at which a solid starts melting. This temperature is called the melting point of that solid.
cards.text_html: Melting point: A constant temperature at which a solid starts melting. This temperature is called the melting point of that solid.

cards.question: Freezing: A process in which liquid changes into a solid form is called freezing.
cards.text_html: Freezing: A process in which liquid changes into a solid form is called freezing.

cards.question: Force: A push or a pull acting on a body that tends to change its state of rest or motion is called a force.
cards.text_html: Force: A push or a pull acting on a body that tends to change its state of rest or motion is called a force.

cards.question: Change in state of matter: Heating and cooling help us bring about a change in the state of matter. For example, on heating ice changes to water, and water changes to steam. Similarly, on cooling steam changes to water, and water changes to ice.
cards.text_html: Change in state of matter: Heating and cooling help us bring about a change in the state of matter. For example, on heating ice changes to water, and water changes to steam. Similarly, on cooling steam changes to water, and water changes to ice.

cards.question: ​​​​​​​ Burning on heating: Whenever a combustible substance is heated, it started burning. Light a candle and observe it. After some time, we see that the size of the candle changes. The changes are irreversible.
cards.text_html: ​​​​​​​Burning on heating: Whenever a combustible substance is heated, it started burning. Light a candle and observe it. After some time, we see that the size of the candle changes. The changes are irreversible.

cards.question: Pressure: Pressure can cause many kinds of changes. Changes in shape and size: if we press fluffy solids their shapes change. When the pressure is released, they get back to their original shape. Breaking object: when pressure is applied to a brittle substance, they break into pieces. It is an irreversible change.
cards.text_html:
Pressure: Pressure can cause many kinds of changes.
  1. Changes in shape and size: if we press fluffy solids their shapes change. When the pressure is released, they get back to their original shape.
  2. Breaking object: when pressure is applied to a brittle substance, they break into pieces. It is an irreversible change.

cards.question: The Blooming of flowers is an irreversible change because flowers cannot change back into buds.
cards.text_html: The Blooming of flowers is an irreversible change because flowers cannot change back into buds.

cards.question: Ripening fruits is an irreversible change because it is not possible to get back the raw fruits from ripened or mature ones.
cards.text_html: Ripening fruits is an irreversible change because it is not possible to get back the raw fruits from ripened or mature ones.

cards.question: Milk gets spoiled when not refrigerated, particularly in summer. This is called curdling or souring of milk, which is an irreversible change.
cards.text_html: Milk gets spoiled when not refrigerated, particularly in summer. This is called curdling or souring of milk, which is an irreversible change.

cards.question: Burning of paper is an irreversible change.
cards.text_html: Burning of paper is an irreversible change.

cards.question: Cooking food is an irreversible change because we cannot get back the ingredients in their original form after cooking them. For example, after a cake is baked using flour, egg, milk, chocolate, etc., we cannot get back the ingredients.
cards.text_html: Cooking food is an irreversible change because we cannot get back the ingredients in their original form after cooking them. For example, after a cake is baked using flour, egg, milk, chocolate, etc., we cannot get back the ingredients.

cards.question: Expansion And Contraction Of Materials: Some materials expand on heating and some contract on cooling.
cards.text_html:
Expansion And Contraction Of Materials:
Some materials expand on heating and some contract on cooling.

cards.question: Applications of Expansion and Contraction: The jammed metal lid of a jam jar can be opened by heating. The jar is inverted and just the lid is dipped in hot water. After some time, the lid can be opened easily as the lid gets slightly expanded.
cards.text_html:
Applications of Expansion and Contraction:
The jammed metal lid of a jam jar can be opened by heating. The jar is inverted and just the lid is dipped in hot water. After some time, the lid can be opened easily as the lid gets slightly expanded.
